heading: Unified Carrier Registration System plan and agreement
label: 14504a Unified Carrier Registration System plan and agreement

children.label: i
children.text: for calendar years 2008 and 2009, has the meaning given the term in section 31101; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for years beginning after , means a self-propelled vehicle described in section 31101.December 31, 2009

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: B .—Exception
children.text: With respect to determining the size of a motor carrier or motor private carrier’s fleet in calculating the fee to be paid by a motor carrier or motor private carrier pursuant to subsection (f)(1), the motor carrier or motor private carrier shall have the option to include, in addition to commercial motor vehicles as defined in subparagraph (A), any self-propelled vehicle used on the highway in commerce to transport passengers or property for compensation regardless of the gross vehicle weight rating of the vehicle or the number of passengers transported by such vehicle.

children.label: i
children.text: that is in compliance with the requirements of subsection (e); and

children.label: ii
children.text: in which the motor carrier, motor private carrier, broker, freight forwarder, or leasing company to which the agreement applies maintains its principal place of business.

children.label: i
children.text: the State in which the motor carrier, motor private carrier, broker, freight forwarder, or leasing company maintains its principal place of business is not in compliance with the requirements of subsection (e); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the motor carrier, motor private carrier, broker, freight forwarder, or leasing company does not have a principal place of business in the United States.

children.heading: .—Intrastate fee
children.label: 3 .—Intrastate fee
children.text: The term “intrastate fee” means any fee, tax, or other type of assessment, including per vehicle fees and gross receipts taxes, imposed on a motor carrier or motor private carrier for the renewal of the intrastate authority or insurance filings of such carrier with a State.

children.heading: .—Leasing company
children.label: 4 .—Leasing company
children.text: The term “leasing company” means a lessor that is engaged in the business of leasing or renting for compensation motor vehicles without drivers to a motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder.

children.label: I
children.text: under subchapter I of chapter 135; or

children.label: II
children.text: through exemption actions by the former Interstate Commerce Commission under this title.

children.label: I
children.text: any carrier subject to section 13504; or

children.label: II
children.text: any other carrier that the board of directors of the unified carrier registration plan determines to be appropriate pursuant to subsection (d)(4)(C).

children.label: i
children.text: under subchapter I of chapter 135; or

children.label: ii
children.text: through exemption actions by the former Interstate Commerce Commission under this title.

children.heading: .—Participating state
children.label: 6 .—Participating state
children.text: The term “participating State” means a State that has complied with the requirements of subsection (e).

children.heading: SSRS.—
children.label: 7 SSRS.—
children.text: The term “SSRS” means the single state registration system in effect on the date of enactment of this section.

children.heading: .—Unified carrier registration agreement
children.label: 8 .—Unified carrier registration agreement
children.text: The terms “unified carrier registration agreement” and “UCR agreement” mean the interstate agreement developed under the unified carrier registration plan governing the collection and distribution of registration and financial responsibility information provided and fees paid by motor carriers, motor private carriers, brokers, freight forwarders, and leasing companies pursuant to this section.

children.heading: .—Unified carrier registration plan
children.label: 9 .—Unified carrier registration plan
children.text: The terms “unified carrier registration plan” and “UCR plan” mean the organization of State, Federal, and industry representatives responsible for developing, implementing, and administering the unified carrier registration agreement.

children.heading: .—Vehicle registration
children.label: 10 .—Vehicle registration
children.text: The term “vehicle registration” means the registration of any commercial motor vehicle under the International Registration Plan (as defined in section 31701) or any other registration law or regulation of a jurisdiction.

children.heading: .—Applicability of Provisions to Freight Forwarders
children.label: b .—Applicability of Provisions to Freight Forwarders
children.text: A freight forwarder that operates commercial motor vehicles and is not required to register as a carrier pursuant to section 13903(b) shall be subject to the provisions of this section as if the freight forwarder is a motor carrier.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: the registration with the State of the interstate operations of the motor carrier or motor private carrier;

children.label: B
children.text: the filing with the State of information relating to the financial responsibility of a motor carrier or motor private carrier pursuant to sections 31138 or 31139;

children.label: C
children.text: the filing with the State of the name of the local agent for service of process of the motor carrier or motor private carrier pursuant to section 503 or 13304; or

children.label: i
children.text: registered under section 13902 or section 13905(b); and

children.label: I
children.text: intrastate service provided by motor carriers of passengers that is not subject to the preemption provisions of section 14501(a);

children.label: II
children.text: motor carriers of property, motor private carriers, brokers, or freight forwarders, or their services or operations, that are described in subparagraphs (B) and (C) of section 14501(c)(2); and

children.label: III
children.text: the intrastate transportation of waste or recyclable materials by any carrier; or

to require any interstate motor carrier or motor private carrier that also performs intrastate operations to pay any fee or tax which a carrier engaged exclusively in intrastate operations is exempt.2So in original.2

children.heading: .—Governance of plan; establishment
children.label: A .—Governance of plan; establishment
children.text: The unified carrier registration plan shall have a board of directors consisting of representatives of the Department of Transportation, participating States, and the motor carrier industry. The Secretary shall establish the board.

children.heading: .—Federal motor carrier safety administration
children.label: i .—Federal motor carrier safety administration
children.text: One director from each of the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration’s 4 service areas (as those areas were defined by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration on ) from among the chief administrative officers of the State agencies responsible for overseeing the administration of the UCR agreement.January 1, 2005

children.heading: .—State agencies
children.label: ii .—State agencies
children.text: Five directors from the professional staffs of State agencies responsible for overseeing the administration of the UCR agreement in their respective States. Nominees for these 5 directorships shall be submitted to the Secretary by the national association of professional employees of the State agencies responsible for overseeing the administration of the UCR agreement in their respective States.

children.heading: .—Motor carrier industry
children.label: iii .—Motor carrier industry
children.text: Five directors from the motor carrier industry. At least 1 of the appointees under this clause shall be a representative of a national trade association representing the general motor carrier of property industry. At least 1 of the appointees under this clause shall represent a motor carrier that falls within the smallest fleet fee bracket.

children.heading: .—Department of transportation
children.label: iv .—Department of transportation
children.text: The Deputy Administrator of the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration, or such other presidential appointee from the Department, as the Secretary may appoint.

children.heading: .—Chairperson and vice-chairperson
children.label: C .—Chairperson and vice-chairperson
children.text: The Secretary shall designate 1 director as chairperson and 1 director as vice-chairperson of the board. The chairperson and vice-chairperson shall serve in such capacity for the term of their appointment as directors.

children.heading: .—Initial terms
children.label: i .—Initial terms
children.text: In appointing the initial board, the Secretary shall designate 5 of the appointed directors for initial terms of 3 years, 5 of the appointed directors for initial terms of 2 years, and 5 of the appointed directors for initial terms of 1 year.

children.heading: .—Thereafter
children.label: ii .—Thereafter
children.text: After the initial term, all directors shall be appointed for terms of 3 years; except that the term of the Deputy Administrator or other individual designated by the Secretary under subparagraph (B)(iv) shall be at the discretion of the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Succession
children.label: iii .—Succession
children.text: A director may be appointed to succeed himself or herself.

children.heading: .—End of service
children.label: iv .—End of service
children.text: A director may continue to serve on the board until his or her successor is appointed.

children.heading: .—Rules and regulations governing the ucr agreement
children.label: 2 .—Rules and regulations governing the ucr agreement
i
children.text: the annual submission of the information required by a base-State of a motor carrier, motor private carrier, leasing company, broker, or freight forwarder;

children.label: ii
children.text: the transmission of information by a participating State to the Unified Carrier Registration System;

children.label: iii
children.text: the payment of excess fees by a State to the designated depository and the distribution of fees by the depository to those States so entitled; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the providing of notice by a motor carrier, motor private carrier, broker, freight forwarder, or leasing company to the board of the intent of such entity to change its base-State, and the procedures for a State to object to such a change under subparagraph (C);

children.label: B
children.text: provide for the administration of the unified carrier registration agreement, including procedures for amending the agreement and obtaining clarification of any provision of the Agreement;

children.label: C
children.text: provide procedures for dispute resolution under the agreement that provide due process for all involved parties; and

children.label: D
children.text: designate a depository.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except for the representative of the Department appointed under paragraph (1)(B)(iv), no director shall receive any compensation or other benefits from the Federal Government for serving on the board or be considered a Federal employee as a result of such service.

children.heading: .—Expenses
children.label: B .—Expenses
children.text: All directors shall be reimbursed for expenses they incur attending meetings of the board. In addition, the board may approve the reimbursement of expenses incurred by members of any subcommittee or task force appointed under paragraph (5) for carrying out the duties of the subcommittee or task force. The reimbursement of expenses to directors and subcommittee and task force members shall be under subchapter II of chapter 57 of title 5, United States Code, governing reimbursement of expenses for travel by Federal employees.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The board shall meet at least once per year. Additional meetings may be called, as needed, by the chairperson of the board, a majority of the directors, or the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Quorum
children.label: B .—Quorum
children.text: A majority of directors shall constitute a quorum.

children.heading: .—Voting
children.label: C .—Voting
children.text: Approval of any matter before the board shall require the approval of a majority of all directors present at the meeting, except that a decision to approve the exclusion of carriers from the definition of the term “motor carrier” under subsection (a)(5) shall require an affirmative vote of ¾ of all such directors..2

children.heading: .—Open meetings
children.label: D .—Open meetings
children.text: Meetings of the board and any subcommittees or task forces appointed under paragraph (5) shall be subject to the provisions of .section 552b of title 5

children.heading: .—Industry advisory subcommittee
children.label: A .—Industry advisory subcommittee
children.text: The chairperson shall appoint an industry advisory subcommittee. The industry advisory subcommittee shall consider any matter before the board and make recommendations to the board.

children.heading: .—Other subcommittees
children.label: B .—Other subcommittees
children.text: The chairperson shall appoint an audit subcommittee, a dispute resolution subcommittee, and any additional subcommittees and task forces that the board determines to be necessary.

children.heading: .—Membership
children.label: C .—Membership
children.text: The chairperson of each subcommittee shall be a director. The other members of subcommittees and task forces may be directors or nondirectors.

children.heading: .—Representation on subcommittees
children.label: D .—Representation on subcommittees
children.text: Except for the industry advisory subcommittee (the membership of which shall consist solely of representatives of entities subject to the fee requirements of subsection (f)), each subcommittee and task force shall include representatives of the participating States and the motor carrier industry.

children.heading: .—Delegation of authority
children.label: 6 .—Delegation of authority
children.text: The board may contract with any person or any agency of a State to perform administrative functions required under the unified carrier registration agreement, but may not delegate its decision or policy-making responsibilities.

children.label: i
children.text: the administrative costs associated with the unified carrier registration plan and the agreement;

children.label: ii
children.text: whether the revenues generated in the previous year and any surplus or shortage from that or prior years enable the participating States to achieve the revenue levels set by the board; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the provisions governing fees under subsection (f)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: within 90 days after receiving the board’s recommendation under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: after notice and opportunity for public comment.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual was acting within the scope of his or her responsibilities as a director; and

children.label: B
children.text: the harm was not caused by willful or criminal misconduct, gross negligence, reckless misconduct, or a conscious, flagrant indifference to the right or safety of the party harmed by the individual.

children.heading: 105.—Inapplicability of chapterof title
children.label: 9 105.—Inapplicability of chapterof title
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to the unified carrier registration plan, the board, or its committees.

children.heading: .—Certain fees not affected
children.label: 10 .—Certain fees not affected
children.text: This section does not limit the amount of money a State may charge for vehicle registration or the amount of any fuel use tax a State may impose pursuant to the International Fuel Tax Agreement (as defined in section 31701).

children.label: A
children.text: identifying the State agency that has or will have the legal authority, resources, and qualified personnel necessary to administer the agreement in accordance with the rules and regulations promulgated by the board of directors; and

children.label: B
children.text: demonstrating that an amount at least equal to the revenue derived by the State from the unified carrier registration agreement shall be used for motor carrier safety programs, enforcement, or the administration of the UCR plan and UCR agreement.

children.heading: .—Amended plans
children.label: 2 .—Amended plans
children.text: A State that submits a plan under this subsection may change the agency designated in the plan by filing an amended plan with the Secretary and the chairperson of the board of directors.

children.heading: .—Withdrawal of plan
children.label: 3 .—Withdrawal of plan
children.text: If a State withdraws, or notifies the Secretary that it is withdrawing, the plan it submitted under this subsection, the State may no longer participate in the unified carrier registration agreement or receive any portion of the revenues derived under the agreement. The Secretary shall notify the chairperson upon receiving notice from a State that it is withdrawing its plan or withdrawing from the agreement, or both.

children.heading: .—Termination of eligibility
children.label: 4 .—Termination of eligibility
children.text: If a State fails to submit a plan to the Secretary in accordance with paragraph (1) or withdraws its plan under paragraph (3), the State may not submit or resubmit a plan or participate in the agreement.

children.heading: .—Provision of plan to chairperson
children.label: 5 .—Provision of plan to chairperson
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a copy of each plan submitted under this subsection to the chairperson of the board of directors not later than 10 days after date of submission of the plan.

children.heading: .—Contents of Unified Carrier Registration Agreement
children.label: f .—Contents of Unified Carrier Registration Agreement
i
children.text: to a motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder under the UCR agreement shall be based on the number of commercial motor vehicles owned or operated by the motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to a broker or leasing company under the UCR agreement shall be equal to the smallest fee charged to a motor carrier, motor private carrier, and freight forwarder under this paragraph.

children.label: B
children.text: The fees shall be determined by the Secretary based upon the recommendation of the board under subsection (d)(7).

children.label: C
children.text: The board shall develop for purposes of charging fees no more than 6 and no less than 4 brackets of carriers (including motor private carriers) based on the size of fleet.

children.label: D
children.text: The fee scale shall be progressive in the amount of the fee.

children.label: i
children.text: are insufficient to provide the revenues to which the States are entitled under this section; or

children.label: ii
children.text: exceed those revenues.

children.heading: .—Determination of ownership or operation
children.label: 2 .—Determination of ownership or operation
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a commercial motor vehicle is owned or operated by a motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder if the vehicle is registered under Federal law or State law, or both, in the name of the motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder or is controlled by the motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder under a long term lease during a vehicle registration year.

children.heading: .—Calculation of number of commercial motor vehicles owned or operated
children.label: 3 .—Calculation of number of commercial motor vehicles owned or operated
children.text: The number of commercial motor vehicles owned or operated by a motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder for purposes of paragraph (1) shall be based either on the number of commercial motor vehicles the motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder has indicated it operates on its most recently filed MCS–150 or the total number of such vehicles it owned or operated for the 12-month period ending on June 30 of the year immediately prior to the registration year of the Unified Carrier Registration System. A motor carrier may include in the calculation of its fleet size for purposes of paragraph (1) any commercial motor vehicle. Motor carriers and motor private carriers in the calculation of their fleet size for purposes of paragraph (1) may elect not to include commercial motor vehicles used exclusively in the intrastate transportation of property, waste, or recyclable material.

children.heading: .—Payment of fees
children.label: 4 .—Payment of fees
children.text: Motor carriers, motor private carriers, leasing companies, brokers, and freight forwarders shall pay all fees required under this section to their base-State pursuant to the UCR Agreement.

A State that participated in the SSRS in the last registration year under the SSRS ending before the date of enactment of the Unified Carrier Registration Act of 2005 and complies with subsection (e) is entitled to receive under this section a portion of the revenues generated under the UCR agreement equivalent to the revenues it received under the SSRS in such last registration year, as long as the State continues to comply with subsection (e).

A State that collected intrastate registration fees from interstate motor carriers, interstate motor private carriers, or interstate exempt carriers and complies with subsection (e) is entitled to receive under this section an additional portion of the revenues generated under the UCR agreement equivalent to the revenues it received from such carriers in the last calendar year ending before the date of enactment of the Unified Carrier Registration Act of 2005, as long as the State continues to comply with subsection (e).

States that comply with subsection (e) but did not participate in SSRS during such last registration year shall be entitled under this section to an annual allotment not to exceed $500,000 from the revenues generated under the UCR agreement, as long as the State continues to comply with the provisions of subsection (e).
The amount of revenues generated under the UCR agreement to which a State is entitled under this section shall be calculated by the board and approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Distribution of UCR Agreement Revenues.—
children.label: h Distribution of UCR Agreement Revenues.—

children.heading: .—Eligibility
children.label: 1 .—Eligibility
children.text: Each State that is in compliance with subsection (e) shall be entitled under this section to a portion of the revenues derived from the UCR Agreement in accordance with subsection (g).

children.heading: .—Entitlement to revenues
children.label: 2 .—Entitlement to revenues
children.text: A State that is in compliance with subsection (e) may retain an amount of the gross revenues it collects from motor carriers, motor private carriers, brokers, freight forwarders and leasing companies under the UCR agreement equivalent to the portion of revenues to which the State is entitled under subsection (g). All revenues a participating State collects in excess of the amount to which the State is so entitled shall be forwarded to the depository designated by the board under subsection (d)(2)(D).

children.label: A
children.text: On a pro rata basis to each participating State that did not collect revenues under the UCR agreement equivalent to the amount such State is entitled under subsection (g), except that the sum of the gross revenues collected under the UCR agreement by a participating State and the amount distributed to it from the depository shall not exceed the amount to which the State is entitled under subsection (g).

children.label: B
children.text: After all distributions under subparagraph (A) have been made, to pay the administrative costs of the UCR plan and the UCR agreement.

children.heading: .—Retention of certain excess funds
children.label: 4 .—Retention of certain excess funds
children.text: Any excess funds held by the depository after distributions and payments under paragraphs (3)(A) and (3)(B) shall be retained in the depository, and the fees charged under the UCR agreement to motor carriers, motor private carriers, leasing companies, freight forwarders, and brokers for the next fee year shall be reduced by the Secretary accordingly.

children.heading: .—Civil actions
children.label: 1 .—Civil actions
children.text: Upon request by the Secretary, the Attorney General may bring a civil action in the United States district court described in paragraph (2) to enforce an order issued to require compliance with this section and with the terms of the UCR agreement.

children.heading: .—Venue
children.label: 2 .—Venue
children.text: An action under this section may be brought only in a United States district court in the State in which compliance with the order is required.

children.heading: .—Relief
children.label: 3 .—Relief
children.text: Subject to , the court, on a proper showing shall issue a temporary restraining order or a preliminary or permanent injunction requiring that the State or any person comply with this section.section 1341 of title 28

children.label: i
children.text: submit information documents as required under subsection (d)(2); or

children.label: ii
children.text: pay the fees required under subsection (f); or

children.label: B
children.text: authorizes a State to require a motor carrier, motor private carrier, or freight forwarder to display as evidence of compliance any form of identification in excess of those permitted under section 14506 on or in a commercial motor vehicle.

children.heading: .—Application to Intrastate Carriers
children.label: j .—Application to Intrastate Carriers
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, a State may elect to apply the provisions of the UCR agreement to motor carriers and motor private carriers and freight forwarders subject to its jurisdiction that operate solely in intrastate commerce within the borders of the State.
